text: A recipe uses flour and sugar in the ratio 3:1. If Sam uses 9 cups of flour, how many cups of sugar does he need?
generatorSolution: The ratio of flour to sugar is 3:1. This means for every 3 cups of flour, Sam needs 1 cup of sugar. Sam uses 9 cups of flour. To find how many groups of 3 are in 9, we divide: 9 ÷ 3 = 3. So he needs 3 × 1 = 3 cups of sugar.
